assertionResults.observation: Guests were redirected from /admin to /admin/login, the normal user was bounced away from /admin to /, and the admin user could access /admin/orders.
Buyer Protection was visible on home and football detail pages, but the authenticated account surface at /my/tickets returned a 404 instead of showing purchase-trust content.
Buyer Protection copy clearly states a 100% Money-Back Guarantee and exposes a buyer protection policy action plus customer service/support language.
Relevant public football surfaces rendered trust badges including Verified, Buyer Protected, Secure Delivery, and Official on detail pages.
Trust badges appeared on football/event pages, while the home goods page only showed Buyer Protection copy without the event-only badges, demonstrating conditional rendering by surface/item type.
On the mobile football detail page, Official/Buyer Protected/Secure Delivery badges and Buyer Protection copy sat immediately above the For sale and Purchase CTAs.
Refund/guarantee/support messaging was present near purchase CTAs on the football detail page with 100% Guarantee and 24/7 Support blocks adjacent to the action buttons.
Could not verify account/order trust messaging because /my/tickets rendered a 404 after user login instead of an account/order surface.

Trust naming was not fully consistent: the home page uses Buyer Protection + 100% Money-Back Guarantee, while the detail page adds Official and a separate 100% Guarantee phrasing; account copy could not be compared because the account route 404s.
Across the reachable browse and detail journey, trust statements consistently promised buyer protection, guarantee coverage, and support without contradicting delivery/refund claims.
Support/trust copy was visible during the guest-to-detail purchase journey before login gating, including Buyer Protection, guarantee, and 24/7 support messaging.
Admin gating stayed intact across guest, non-admin, and admin checks, with only the admin account able to reach an admin page (/admin/orders).

frictions.description: The buyer protection policy button overlays the first football card and blocks direct clicks until dismissed.
frictions.resolution: Clicked the buyer protection policy button first, then selected the football event card.

blockers.description: Authenticated account evidence was incomplete because /my/tickets returned a 404 instead of an account/order surface.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried navigation after successful user login; route consistently rendered 404.

blockers.description: The canonical /admin route itself returned 404 even for the authenticated admin; admin validation had to use /admin/orders instead.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Logged in via /admin/login and retried /admin before validating access on /admin/orders.

summary: Tested 12 assigned assertions: 8 passed and 4 failed. Main issues were the missing /my/tickets account surface and inconsistent/unfinished trust coverage across account/admin canonical routes.
